conflicts.description: SAP inventory sync is specified as both real-time and daily batch process

version_a.text: System must integrate with SAP ERP for real-time inventory synchronization.

version_b.text: System must sync product information from SAP via daily batch process.

conflicts.description: Business rule mandates real-time SAP inventory sync, directly contradicting the daily batch sync requirement

version_a.text: Inventory sync with SAP must be real-time to prevent overselling out-of-stock items.

version_b.text: System must sync product information from SAP via daily batch process.

conflicts.description: Vendor portal allows vendors to manage product listings, but business rule prohibits vendors from setting prices — US-016 implies full listing management including price, conflicting with TR-021

version_a.text: Vendors cannot set product prices; only RetailCorp central admin controls pricing.

version_b.text: The system shall allow vendors to create, update, and manage their own product listings.

conflicts.description: Vendor portal allows vendors to manage product listings, but business rule prohibits vendors from setting prices — TR-020 implies full listing management including price, conflicting with TR-021

version_a.text: Vendors cannot set product prices; only RetailCorp central admin controls pricing.

version_b.text: Vendors must be able to manage their product listings and inventory via portal.

conflicts.description: Real-time inventory sync with SAP conflicts with the daily batch sync functional requirement, also contradicted by US-015 reaffirming real-time

version_a.text: The system shall sync inventory levels with SAP in real time to reflect accurate stock availability.

version_b.text: System must sync product information from SAP via daily batch process.

conflicts.description: GDPR compliance is required for NRI customer data privacy, but all data must be stored on servers physically within India — GDPR may require data portability and deletion rights that conflict with strict India-only data residency enforcement, and GDPR is an EU regulation not directly applicable to India-stored data

version_a.text: Platform must comply with GDPR regulations to support NRI customer data privacy.

version_b.text: All customer data must be stored on servers physically located within India.

conflicts.description: ZestMoney partnership may not be finalized before development, yet no corresponding functional requirement excludes it — assumption implies a dependency that may block a payment feature if included in scope

version_a.text: ZestMoney partnership agreement may not be finalized before development begins.

version_b.text: The system shall support payment via credit card, debit card, UPI, net banking, and EMI.

conflicts.description: Peak concurrent user assumption may underestimate actual online traffic, conflicting with the fixed 50,000 concurrent user non-functional requirement used for capacity planning

version_a.text: Platform must handle at least 50,000 concurrent users during peak sale events.

version_b.text: 50,000 peak users is based on physical store footfall and may underestimate online traffic.

gaps.message: 'Business Context and Background' has limited coverage.
'Project Objectives and Success Criteria' has limited coverage.
'User Journeys and Use Cases' has limited coverage.
